text: For humans to hold the responsibility to be a representative of Allah SWT in this world there has to be a communication. This is where as a parent, every human being has to communicate or speak to the child from the earliest age of childhood especially on matters concerning Islam. Islam is an identity of mankind, as well as their source of ethics and their guide to haq (truth). As an identity, the religion has a great influence over the mankind, which then acts as their spiritual guidance towards achieving Allah SWT’s wish in their lives. The Islamic education in the family is of great significant because it helps the children to increase their sense of morality so that would ward them from indecent and prohibited acts and behaviour, which might jeopardize their future in this life and hereafter. Children go through tremendous development growth both physically and mentally at very early ages of their lives. This makes it important that children are guided to learn from an early stage in their lives. Therefore, this study is based on qualitative approach and used interviews and documentation methods for data collection. Some of the findings from this study were: From the psychology perspective there are four developmental theories in early childhood, namely: i. Social Development Theory states that the main place where humans learn is from their social environment. They behave based on what they believe is their capability to do things or the outcomes of the things they do. ii. Cognitive Development Theory explain the process of thinking or assessing something which enables them to link, evaluate and judge various things around them. Based on age there are four cognitive developmental stages which are: a. Stage of sensorimotor (sensory), b. stage of preoperational, c. stage of concrete operational and d. stage of formal operational. iv. Constructivism theory is a learning theory in which teachers persuade students to do their own discover and construction of knowledge and also to develop their abilities and skills by doing things independently on their own.
headline: Fully Human Written
